goal: Upgrade the canonical reference-project pipeline from source intelligence to implementation-ready Herdr integration intelligence without changing product code

ontology.system_type: evidence-backed agentic research and integration-intelligence pipeline

components.name: source intelligence P0-P7
components.responsibility: freeze, index, map, verify, classify, publish and audit the reference source

components.name: Herdr current-state cartography P8
components.responsibility: map fresh target layers, authority seams, tests and documentation drift

components.name: behavior gap analysis P9
components.responsibility: classify every observable reference behavior against current Herdr

components.name: data authority map P10
components.responsibility: trace provenance and classify server, transport, client, absent or out-of-scope data

components.name: cell-level fidelity P11
components.responsibility: specify deterministic layout, cells, hit regions, fallbacks, motion and performance baselines

components.name: component integration blueprint P12
components.responsibility: bind macro, micro and overlay components to Herdr state/view/render/input/focus ownership

components.name: implementation and verification plan P13
components.responsibility: derive TDD slices and local, cross, capability, benchmark and soak obligations

components.name: integration audit and skill routing P14
components.responsibility: validate traceability, fidelity, architecture, isolation and terminal status

dependencies.kind: derives required data from accepted behavior
derives fixtures from accepted visible behavior
binds component geometry and visual states

claims.text: P0-P7 and P8 are separated so desired source architecture cannot overwrite current Herdr reality
Every source behavior is classified against current Herdr before datum or component design
Every desired datum has provenance, authority, transport, freshness, failure, retention and verification
Exact terminal fidelity uses deterministic cell-level fixtures with zero undeclared differences and explicit capability profiles
Component design occurs only after behavior, data and layout contracts and records state/view/render/input/focus ownership
Implementation planning is RED-first and includes local and cross-layer failure verification
P14 passes only with complete references, integration V=0, zero undeclared fidelity differences and product isolation
The current Herdr architecture has explicit shared event/subscription seams and pure geometry-to-render separation but no graph-proven orchestration run, todo, token usage or cost model

sources.path: home-ayaz-projects-herdr exact symbol and graph-augmented source queries at a61cfb640c315cff89df772acf0b5dec4111319b

verifications.expected: 15 phases and all source contracts valid

verifications.command: Codebase Memory architecture plus exact Herdr symbol queries
verifications.expected: fresh target layers and seams resolve
provenance, authority, stale, failure and verification fields present
terminal-cell, viewport, capability and zero-diff contracts present
RED-first local and cross-test obligations present

verifications.command: unit tests, pipeline validator, JSON parse, eval coverage and system-map validator
